2604.11304.title: BankerToolBench: Evaluating AI Agents in End-to-End Investment Banking Workflows
2604.11304.paper_summary: Existing AI benchmarks lack the fidelity to assess economically meaningful progress on professional workflows. To evaluate frontier AI agents in a high-value, labor-intensive profession, we introduce BankerToolBench (BTB): an open-source benchmark of end-to-end analytical workflows routinely performed by junior investment bankers. To develop an ecologically valid benchmark grounded in representative work environments, we collaborated with 502 investment bankers from leading firms. BTB requires agents to execute senior banker requests by navigating data rooms, using industry tools (market data platform, SEC filings database), and generating multi-file deliverables--including Excel financial models, PowerPoint pitch decks, and PDF/Word reports. Completing a BTB task takes bankers up to 21 hours, underscoring the economic stakes of successfully delegating this work to AI. BTB enables automated evaluation of any LLM or agent, scoring deliverables against 100+ rubric criteria defined by veteran investment bankers to capture stakeholder utility. Testing 9 frontier models, we find that even the best-performing model (GPT-5.4) fails nearly half of the rubric criteria and bankers rate 0% of its outputs as client-ready. Our failure analysis reveals key obstacles (such as breakdowns in cross-artifact consistency) and improvement directions for agentic AI in high-stakes professional workflows.
2604.11304.paper_summary_zh: 現有的人工智慧基準缺乏評估專業工作流程中具有經濟意義的進展的準確性。為了評估在高價值、勞動密集型職業中的前沿人工智慧代理，我們推出了BankerToolBench (BTB)：一個開源的基準，涵蓋初級投資銀行家日常執行的端到端分析工作流程。為了開發一個基於代表性工作環境的生態有效基準，我們與來自領先公司的502名投資銀行家合作。BTB要求代理通過導航數據室、使用行業工具（市場數據平台、SEC檔案數據庫）來執行高級銀行家的請求，並生成多文件交付物——包括Excel財務模型、PowerPoint推介文稿和PDF/Word報告。完成一項BTB任務需要銀行家最多21小時，突顯了成功將這項工作委派給人工智慧的經濟風險。BTB使任何大型語言模型或代理的自動評估成為可能，根據資深投資銀行家定義的100多項標準對交付物進行打分，以捕捉利益相關者的效用。測試9個前沿模型時，我們發現即使是表現最佳的模型（GPT-5.4）也未能滿足近一半的標準，且銀行家對其輸出的客戶準備度評分為0%。我們的失敗分析揭示了關鍵障礙（例如跨工件一致性崩潰）以及在高風險專業工作流程中對代理人工智慧的改進方向。
2604.11304.author: Elaine Lau et.al.
2604.11304.authors: Elaine Lau, Markus Dücker, Ronak Chaudhary, Hui Wen Goh, Rosemary Wei, Vaibhav Kumar, Saed Qunbar, Guram Gogia, Yi Liu, Scott Millslagle, Nasim Borazjanizadeh, Ulyana Tkachenko, Samuel Eshun Danquah, Collin Schweiker, Vijay Karumathil, Asrith Devalaraju, Varsha Sandadi, Haemi Nam, Punit Arani, Ray Epps, Abdullah Arif, Sahil Bhaiwala, Curtis Northcutt, Skyler Wang, Anish Athalye, Jonas Mueller, Francisco Guzmán
